summary.intent: Add 100+ default bundled skills to Moltis (ported from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw), embedded in the binary like web UI assets, with category metadata, web UI filtering, and a prompt format fallback system.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented a complete bundled skills system: 101 skills embedded via include_dir!, category metadata extracted from directory structure, web UI category filter pills, three-tier prompt format fallback, origin provenance tracking, and full PR review cycle with all comments resolved.

repo: Moltis uses include_dir! for embedding static assets at compile time — skills follow the same pattern as crates/web/src/assets/
Skills are discovered via a priority chain: Project > Personal > Registry > Plugin > Bundled — user skills override bundled by name
SkillSource enum is used exhaustively in discover.rs and watcher.rs — adding a new variant requires updating all match arms
crates/skills/src/discover.rs has a FsSkillDiscoverer that needed a CompositeSkillDiscoverer wrapper to merge FS + bundled sources
The gateway forwards features like bundled-skills through a chain: skills -> tools -> gateway -> cli Cargo.toml
Hermes mlops skills use 3-level nesting (mlops/training/axolotl/) requiring recursive directory traversal in discovery
Directory name must match frontmatter 'name' field — 6 skills had mismatches causing silent read failures
normalize_relative_skill_file_path() must be applied to sidecar paths to prevent path traversal in dev-mode filesystem fallback
generate_skills_prompt() is called from append_skills_section in post_state.rs — single call site makes adding max_chars budget straightforward
red-teaming/godmode skill should not be bundled by default — security-sensitive skills require explicit user opt-in

code.finding: BundledSkillStore uses include_dir! in release mode and filesystem fallback in dev mode, identical pattern to crates/web/src/assets.rs
CompositeSkillDiscoverer must use seen.insert() (not !seen.contains()) to deduplicate within the bundled set itself, not just across FS vs bundled
Three-tier fallback: full (name+desc+attrs) -> compact (self-closing, no desc) -> truncated (binary search for largest prefix fitting budget). Default budget 30KB covers ~300+ skills at full format
ReadSkillTool needs with_bundled() constructor and read_bundled() path; sidecar reads must call normalize_relative_skill_file_path() before passing to store
Exhaustive match on SkillSource — Bundled arm is a no-op (bundled skills are not file-watched)
CDN script tags require integrity= SRI hash attribute — CodeQL flags CDN without integrity checks

workflow: Python script for injecting YAML frontmatter is more reliable than sed for multi-line structured content
First sed-based origin injection attempt corrupted files by truncating content — required re-copy from source
cargo check is sufficient for validating compilation when disk space prevents full test compilation (llama-cpp dependency is large)
Running tests with --test bundled and filtering specific test names helps validate feature-specific behavior quickly
Writing a test that iterates all discovered skills and calls read_skill() on each is a high-value integration test that catches name/directory mismatches
GitHub GraphQL API (gh api graphql) is needed to resolve PR review threads — REST API doesn't expose thread resolution
Background tasks (run_in_background) for git push can complete before the foreground response reads output — always check task output file

friction: First sed-based frontmatter injection corrupted skill files by truncating content — required full re-copy from Hermes and rewrite using Python
Disk space issues in worktree prevented running gateway tests (llama-cpp compilation requires significant space)
Background task output file polling via jq failed with parse errors when Greptile review wasn't yet available
Resolving GitHub PR review threads required GraphQL mutations — word splitting in bash required careful quoting of thread IDs
Skills crate watcher.rs had an exhaustive match that wasn't caught until gateway compilation — skills crate compiled fine but gateway failed
Arc import was already in scope in services.rs but std::sync::Arc::new() fully-qualified usage triggered lint warnings after edits
CompositeSkillDiscoverer deduplication bug (within-bundled duplicates) was only caught by Greptile review, not by existing tests
open_items: Some skills still reference ~/.hermes/ paths in GitHub auth skills — cosmetic cleanup deferred
OpenClaw 'extensions/' platform-specific skills (Feishu, QQBot, ACP) were skipped — could be useful for those platforms
Prompt format fallback compact mode truncates to largest fitting prefix — could be smarter (e.g., prioritize by category or usage frequency)
Skills with external binary requirements (requires: bins) have no runtime check UI — web UI could show which required tools are installed
~25 additional openclaw skills were deemed openclaw-specific and skipped — worth revisiting which are actually portable
No mechanism yet to update bundled skills when upstream Hermes/OpenClaw repos change — manual copy process
red-teaming/godmode removed from defaults but no opt-in mechanism for power users who want it
